* fix(pipeline): correctly advance status when all agents for current status are done

Root cause: _get_or_reconstruct_pipeline assumed that when the tracking
table's first incomplete agent was in a different status from the board's
current status, it was always in an EARLIER status (board jumped ahead).
This assumption is wrong when the first incomplete agent is in a LATER
status — meaning all agents for the board's CURRENT status are done.

Bug scenario (issue #2912):
- Board shows 'Backlog', but Backlog agents [speckit.specify, designer]
  are both ✅ Done
- First incomplete agent is 'judge' at 'In Progress' (🔄 Active)
- Old code: reconstructs pipeline for 'In Progress', returns pipeline
  with current_agent=judge → check_backlog_issues finds judge not done →
  waits forever → issue never advances from Backlog to Ready
- Fix: checks whether the requested status has any incomplete agents.
  If not, falls through to reconstruct for the requested status, which
  finds all agents done (is_complete=True), triggering status advancement

The fix correctly distinguishes:
- First incomplete is EARLIER (board jumped ahead) → reconstruct for
  that earlier status (existing behavior, preserved)
- First incomplete is LATER (current status fully done) → reconstruct
  for requested status to trigger advancement (new behavior)

Tests: 2 new tests covering both directions, 1619 total passing

* review: avoid caching empty-agent pipeline states

- Skip set_pipeline_state when agents=[] in _reconstruct_pipeline_state
  to prevent empty-agent states from permanently blocking recovery on
  subsequent poll cycles.

Evaluated against PR #2972 review recommendations.

* fix(blocking-queue): dispatch agents when skip/delete activates next issue

When a blocking issue was skipped or deleted on the Projects page, the
blocking queue correctly activated the next pending issue(s) but never
triggered agent assignment.  The activated issue sat with queue_status=ACTIVE
and no Copilot agent assigned, so work never started (issue #2926).

Root cause: skip_blocking_issue and delete_blocking_issue endpoints called
bq_service.mark_completed() but ignored the returned list of activated
entries.  The skip endpoint also lacked a session dependency, making it
impossible to access the user's access_token for GitHub API calls.

Fix:
- Add session dependency to skip endpoint
- Capture activated entries from mark_completed() in both endpoints
- Add _dispatch_agents_for_activated() background helper that builds a
  WorkflowContext for each activated issue and calls
  orchestrator.assign_agent_for_status() to start the first agent
- Ensure polling is running so the pipeline advances after assignment
- Fire dispatch as asyncio.create_task so HTTP response returns immediately

Tests: 7 new tests covering skip dispatch, no-activation path, 404,
delete dispatch, GitHub close failure, and the dispatch helper itself.
All 1626 tests pass, ruff clean, pyright clean.

---------

Implements parsing and compilation of Unicode property escapes:
- \p{Name} and \P{Name} (braced form)
- \pX and \PX (single-letter shorthand)

Supports Unicode general categories (L, Lu, Ll, etc.),
script names (Latin, Greek, Cyrillic, etc.), and special

Unicode database versions (unicodedata.category() results change). Normalize
the position count in error comparisons and skip tests where patterns cross
NFA/DFA limits on one Python version but not another.

Also add \p{}/\P{} Unicode property escape to the README feature table.

commit.message:
cfl: add CFL-002 — GamutBoundaryDesc m_NumberOfTriangles*3 signed overflow

Fix signed integer overflow in CIccTagGamutBoundaryDesc::Read() and Write()
at IccTagLut.cpp:5730,5787. m_NumberOfTriangles (icInt32Number, file-controlled)
multiplied by 3 overflows when value exceeds INT_MAX/3 (~715M).

Fix: cast to icUInt32Number before multiplication.
CWE-190 (Integer Overflow or Wraparound).

Found by icc_apply_fuzzer during SSD campaign (2026-03-10).
Both Read() and Write() paths fixed.

Add four specialized AI agents to help with PyMAPDL development:

- Documentation Specialist: Reviews docs, validates docstrings, checks
  spelling/style using vale and codespell. Ensures numpydoc compliance
  and Google documentation style.

- PyMAPDL Developer: Writes features and fixes bugs. Understands
  PyMAPDL architecture (local/remote, grpc/console). Runs pre-commit
  hooks and manages dependencies properly.

- Test Engineer: Maintains ~90% test coverage. Minimizes MAPDL
  instance launches using Mock/patch strategies. Understands test
  modes and conditional test execution.

- Code Reviewer: Comprehensive PR reviews ensuring code quality,
  documentation, and testing standards are met before merge.

Files created:
- AGENTS.md: Primary specification document (498 lines)
- .github/AGENTS_GUIDE.md: Detailed usage guide with examples
- .github/AGENTS_QUICKREF.md: One-page quick reference card
- .github/AGENTS_SUMMARY.md: Implementation summary and overview

Files modified:
- README.md: Added AI-Assisted Development section linking to agents

The agents work with any LLM-enabled IDE (GitHub Copilot, Cursor,
JetBrains AI, etc.) and provide consistent, repository-specific
guidance for development tasks.

commit.message:
cfl: retire 62 legacy patches, add CFL-001 (icAnsiToUtf8 HBO fix)

Retire all 62 CFL library patches to patches-retired/ — the patching
system caused repeated multi-hour rework cycles due to context conflicts,
false success claims, and upstream sync overhead. Build.sh now applies
only new targeted patches from cfl/patches/.

CFL-001: Fix heap-buffer-overflow in icAnsiToUtf8 (CWE-125/CWE-170)
- Root cause: CIccTagColorantTable::Read() reads 32-byte name[] without
  null termination. ToXml() passes to icAnsiToUtf8() → strlen() overread.
- Fix 1: Force name[31]='\0' after Read8() in IccTagBasic.cpp
- Fix 2: Defense-in-depth strnlen(szSrc, 256) in icAnsiToUtf8/icUtf8ToAnsi
- PoC: hbo-icAnsiToUtf8-clrt-multitag-IccUtilXml_cpp-Line394.icc
- Verified: patched icc_toxml_fuzzer handles PoC cleanly (exit 0, 0 ASAN)
- iccanalyzer-lite H144 already detects this pattern

Build.sh changes:
- Replaced zero-patch-mode comment with minimal patch loop
- Distinguishes 'applied', 'already applied', and 'FAIL' states
- Cleaned last CFL-083 include remnant from icc_deep_dump_fuzzer.cpp

All 18 fuzzers rebuilt successfully against upstream 1ffa7a8 + CFL-001.
SSD binaries refreshed at /mnt/g/fuzz-ssd/bin/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: discover MA Ingress URL via Supervisor API

In HAOS, localhost:8094 is not reachable from the bridge addon because
each addon runs in its own Docker container. Use the Supervisor API to
discover the MA addon's Docker hostname and ingress_port, then connect
via Docker DNS (e.g. http://d5369777-music-assistant:8094).

Also adds hassio_api and homeassistant_api to ha-addon/config.yaml
so the bridge can query the Supervisor API for addon info.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
